article.title: Effect of chlorhexidine pretreatment on clinical performance of adhesive restorations: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
article.authors: Bourgi R, Holiel AA, Cuevas-Suárez CE
article.abstract: The long-term stability of resin-dentin adhesion remains a major concern in restorative dentistry. Hydrolytic and enzymatic degradation of the hybrid layer contributes to the deterioration of resin-bonded restorations. Inhibition of matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) by chlorhexidine digluconate (CHX) has been proposed to preserve the hybrid layer and improve adhesive durability.
